statement.markdown: Write a function to sort an array of integers in ascending order using a stable bubble sort algorithm. The function should take an array of integers as input and return the sorted array.
statement.notes: The function should use a stable bubble sort algorithm. The input array may contain duplicate elements.

examples.explanation: The input array is sorted in ascending order.

hints.text: Use a stable sorting algorithm like bubble sort.

editorial.title: Stable Bubble Sort
editorial.optimalSolution: A stable bubble sort algorithm makes multiple passes through the array, comparing adjacent elements and swapping them if they are in the wrong order. The algorithm continues until the end of the array is reached or no more swaps are needed.

buggyStarterCode.cpp: bool solve(vector<int>& nums) { for (int i=0; i<nums.size(); i++) for (int j=0; j<nums.size(); j++) { if (nums[j] > nums[j+1]) { swap(nums[j], nums[j+1]); i--; } } return true; }
buggyStarterCode.java: public boolean solve(int[] nums) { for (int i=0; i<nums.length; i++) for (int j=0; j<nums.length; j++) { if (nums[j] > nums[j+1]) { int temp = nums[j]; nums[j] = nums[j+1]; nums[j+1] = temp; i--; } } return true; }
buggyStarterCode.python: def solve(self, nums: list[int]) -> bool: for i in range(len(nums)): for j in range(len(nums)): if nums[j] > nums[j+1]: nums[j], nums[j+1] = nums[j+1], nums[j] i -= 1 return True
buggyStarterCode.javascript: var solve = function(nums) { for (let i=0; i<nums.length; i++) for (let j=0; j<nums.length; j++) { if (nums[j] > nums[j+1]) { let temp = nums[j]; nums[j] = nums[j+1]; nums[j+1] = temp; i--; } } return true; };
